statement.en: ESGE recommends the features detailed in the following list should be confirmed and documented during a surveillance colonoscopy.

subItems.source_span: Hand-extracted from source PDF after careful cross-rec attribution; PDF column flow had scattered these sub-items across the narrative subsection containing R35.

text.en: Assessment of quality of bowel preparation.
Presence of any residual or recurrent adenoma recognized by a transition in pit/vascular pattern (e.g. NICE I to NICE II; Kudo I to Kudo III/IV) (Fig. 3).
The presence of clip artifact. Recognize that clip artifact itself does not need treatment, but, where there is doubt, treatment is safe and prevents unnecessary further procedures for patients.
Targeted biopsies and endoscopic treatment of any potential residual or recurrent adenoma should be performed.
Additional polyps/LNPCPs should be noted and removed if feasible or a plan for their removal made.
